extra.reason: Answered: BFF makes backend CORS config unnecessary for normal operation (same-origin); backend CORS is a hardcoded regex, not env — env only sets the server-side BACKEND_API_URL. See notes + DL11 D1 / T06.

Backend vendored from local source (core/ static copy, no git/clone/submodule); VERSION=0.1.0 seed; serve.py _get_app_version adapted per DL11 D2 (APP_VERSION env then /app/VERSION then v0.0.0-unknown); docker/Dockerfile.backend from local source, context=repo root, non-root uid1000, --workers 1. Verified: pytest 334 passed, ruff clean, docker build OK, /health appVersion==0.1.0, empty-env file-fallback proven, no git in image. prototype/leave_daystate_160h.yaml vendored (byte-identical, git-tracked sibling of core/ in old repo) to keep shipped suite green — ratified.

Dockerfile.web (multi-stage standalone, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_VERSION baked pre-build, non-root), compose.yml (backend host-unbound+health-gated, web only-published, cloudflared TUNNEL_TOKEN env origin->web:3000), Makefile build-wrapper (fail-loud on missing VERSION, build decoupled from runtime env), /api/health passthrough. Gates: host pytest 334, verify-deploy 9/9, frontend lint/type/test/e2e green. Uncommitted (conservative). beads-hook append ratified (survives bd hooks install byte-for-byte).
